refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to the operational period and customer-defined target, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period using telemetry data and compare against the customer-defined availability threshold.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Maintain network link quality to meet availability targets.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Use real-time link health monitoring and predictive analytics.
DP3: Employ adaptive bandwidth management and error correction protocols.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

coupling_check.notes: Redundant links and failover logic are modular and can be encapsulated without tight coupling to other system functions.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of multiple network paths or technologies.
Risk of increased system complexity and cost.
Risk that failover latency may impact teleoperation responsiveness.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating real-time monitoring (DP2) as a complementary DP in future refinement to enhance predictive maintenance.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Responsible for managing teleoperation link availability including network redundancy and failover mechanisms.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connections managed by the Teleoperation module to maintain link availability.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends link status and health data to NGHE Solution and Safety Services for monitoring and alerting.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives teleoperation commands dependent on link availability.

module_containment_scores.notes: High diffusion risk if link management is spread across NGHE Solution; containment is low.
Vehicle module has limited direct control over network link redundancy; poor containment.
Best containment and lowest diffusion risk; Teleoperation module is the natural owner.
Safety Services consumes link status but does not control availability; moderate containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability management within the Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential single point of failure if Teleoperation module is compromised.
Dependency on external network infrastructure not fully controlled by Teleoperation.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate adding monitoring support from Safety Services to improve fault detection and alerting.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR sub-functions and is logically decoupled.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as link management is contained within Teleoperation module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has high containment score and appropriate interfaces to implement the DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and network link monitoring is feasible and aligns with module interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to Teleoperation module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Consider integrating Safety Services for enhanced monitoring and alerting.
Plan for failover latency impact analysis in next refinement.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the requirements with acceptable containment and diffusion risks. Verification and ownership are clear.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Maintain network link quality to meet availability targets.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; next step is to decompose sub-FRs further.

assumptions: Multiple network paths are available for redundancy.
Teleoperation module has control over network failover mechanisms.
open_risks: Failover latency impact on teleoperation responsiveness.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data demonstrating link availability over evaluation period.
